title: toona hexandra var. pilistila
intro: Toona hexandra var. pilistila, also known as the Toona tree, is a deciduous tree native to Southeast Asia, characterized by its large, broad leaves and fragrant flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are derived from its bark, leaves, and roots. The medicinal properties of Toona hexandra var. pilistila have been shown to provide various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory infections. The herbal preparations of this plant typically include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions in traditional medicine.
